content: Does Rust have support for static mutexes yet?
Libstd does. std::sync::Mutex::new is const since rust 1.63. Rust-for-linux technically also has support for it, but you still need to initialize it at runtime before you can use it.
Hm, apparently there's some sort of init_static_sync! macro which looks rather cursed, but I guess I can try using it.

Alice Ryhl said:

Hm, apparently there's some sort of init_static_sync! macro which looks rather cursed, but I guess I can try using it.

What makes it look cursed?

bjorn3 said:

Didn't know that macro existed.

Oddly enough, you reviewed a fix to it :)
So I did. Entirely forgot about that PR.
@Wedson Almeida Filho is this init_static_sync! your own version of the pin-init for static locks? ;-)
Maybe this should be a proc macro and use an attribute syntax so it doesn't look cursed.
well at that point you are just hiding the cursedness xd
Could a proc macro avoid the ".init_array" thing?
I am not too familiar with the hack, but I think we would still need to do it...

A simpler way to avoid .init_array is to be able to emulate the static C initializers, see for example DEFINE_MUTEX -- if we could get the field initializers out of __MUTEX_INITIALIZER and into a Rust const fn, we would have compile-time initialization

Boqun Feng said:

Wedson Almeida Filho is this init_static_sync! your own version of the pin-init for static locks? ;-)

I was trying to convert some C example to Rust and noticed that not being able to have a static mutex was a big hole, so I put this together way before pin-init :)

Wedson Almeida Filho said:

A simpler way to avoid .init_array is to be able to emulate the static C initializers, see for example DEFINE_MUTEX -- if we could get the field initializers out of __MUTEX_INITIALIZER and into a Rust const fn, we would have compile-time initialization

You will still need pin-init in this approach, right? As __MUTEX_INITIALIZER contains a LIST_HEAD_INIT
Not for the mutex. Since it's static, it is pinned
Hmm.. I wonder how would you implement the const fn __MUTEX_INITIALIZER then ;-)

Boqun Feng said:

Hmm.. I wonder how would you implement the const fn __MUTEX_INITIALIZER then ;-)

You're right, we still need pin-init to get a pointer to the object so that we can initialise the self-references

(Sorry for the late response, had missed it on Zulip -- I occasionally get lost with highlighted threads/conversations/streams/whatever-they're-called hiding other entities, these things are too high-tech for me :))
